page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-4 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks, placing in the top 25%. However, its multi-core scaling is relatively weak, falling into the bottom 25% for multi-core stress-ng, Geekbench multi-core, and software compilation tasks. Memory bandwidth is average for large uncached blocks, measuring 51.54 GB/sec read and 38.79 GB/sec write, but weak for small cached blocks. Cryptographic performance is moderate, as evidenced by average scores in Passmark CPU encryption and single-threaded gzip compression.

From a resource density and cost-efficiency perspective, the c2d-standard-4 offers a dedicated hardware profile suitable for workloads that benefit from strong single-threaded execution without requiring massive parallel scaling or local NVMe storage. Given its weak multi-core performance and database operations benchmarks, it is best suited for light web serving, single-threaded text processing, basic background workers, and entry-level general-purpose workloads rather than heavy database hosting or intensive multi-threaded computing.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. The hardware features 2 physical cores with hyper-threading, a 32 MB L3 cache, and lacks local storage or integrated GPUs. Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% in stress-ng tests, alongside average large-block memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression speeds. However, multi-core scaling, database operations, and software compilation benchmarks are comparatively weak. This qualitative resource profile makes the instance cost-efficient for workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation and strong single-threaded execution, such as light web serving, text processing, and basic background tasks, while being less optimal for highly parallelized computing or heavy database workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-4 is a compute-optimized instance with 4 dedicated vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU, offering strong single-core performance for light workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-4 features 4 dedicated vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, delivering strong single-core performance.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC instance delivering strong single-core performance and balanced memory for light compute and web serving workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM.
Strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% of stress-ng benchmarks.
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators.
Average performance in large-block memory bandwidth and single-threaded text processing.
Best suited for light web serving, single-threaded tasks, and basic background workers.
